title: Red Sox took advantage of MLB’s new automated strike challenge rules on Opening Day
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Red Sox utilized MLB's new Automated Ball-Strike Challenge System effectively in their 3-0 Opening Day win over the Reds.
Catcher Carlos Narvaez successfully challenged a strikeout call, helping pitcher Garrett Crochet in the fourth inning.
Roman Anthony's ninth-inning challenge reversal led to a crucial walk, setting up insurance runs for Boston's victory.

title: In first Opening Day with Red Sox, Marcelo Mayer made quite the impression off the bench
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Marcelo Mayer went 2-for-2 as a pinch-hitter, scoring two runs in Red Sox's 3-0 win at Reds.
Mayer, 23, joined the Opening Day roster despite a tough previous season, earning his spot through consistent effort.
Boston pitcher Garrett Crochet's performance limited the Reds to four hits, contributing to the shutout victory.

title: Lucky pup thanks NH firefighters for quick thinking that saved his life
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Ziggy the dog was saved by Campton-Thornton firefighters after cutting his leg on a hidden solar light.
Firefighters applied pressure and a tourniquet to Ziggy's wound, preventing excessive blood loss before reaching the vet.
Ziggy underwent surgery for a cut artery at an animal hospital and is on the path to full recovery.

title: What’s causing my car to burn so much oil?
author: John Paul
bullets: Subaru Legacy owner notices excessive oil consumption near 89,000 miles, Subaru suggests $4,000 short block replacement.
Sticking oil control piston rings and a faulty PCV system can cause high oil consumption in some Subaru engines.
Alternatives to engine replacement include using an engine flush and multiple oil changes to address oil consumption.

title: This Nantucket diner just sold its fifth million dollar prize in the last 2 years
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: The Old South Diner in Nantucket sold its fifth $1 million-plus lottery ticket since March 2024.
The diner received a $20,000 bonus for selling a $2 million winning ticket this month.
Lottery wins include two $2 million tickets, one as recently as March 26, from various games.

title: School bus driver in crash that killed child was allegedly involved in hit-and-run months later
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Jean Charles was arraigned on involuntary manslaughter and motor vehicle homicide charges related to a fatal school bus crash.
Prosecutors allege Charles failed a safety inspection and drove recklessly, leading to the death of 5-year-old Lens Arthur Joseph.
Months after the crash, Charles was allegedly involved in a hit-and-run incident and faces separate charges for driving with a suspended license.

title: 5 takeaways as Garrett Crochet, Marcelo Mayer steer Red Sox to 3-0 Opening Day win over Reds
author: Trevor Hass
bullets: Garrett Crochet pitched six scoreless innings with eight strikeouts, helping the Red Sox to a 3-0 win over the Reds.
Marcelo Mayer hit a crucial double and a single, contributing significantly in the Red Sox's season-opening victory.
Roman Anthony went 3 for 4, leading offensively as the youngest Red Sox outfielder to start Opening Day since 1973.

title: Body of Mass. man missing since escaping from a hospital in 2025 found in lake
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Leonard Mercury, 57, missing since October from Heywood Hospital, found in Crystal Lake, Gardner.
Mercury escaped hospital in mental and medical distress, wearing only hospital clothing, and was shoeless.
Body identified using drones, retrieved by State Police Dive Team; cause of death pending investigation.

title: A doctor once told her not to run. Now she’s taking on the Boston Marathon.
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Sydney Southern to run 2026 Boston Marathon over a decade after femur surgery at Mass General Hospital.
Southern was diagnosed with a giant cell tumor in 2014; advised to limit activity, now cleared for marathon.
She runs to support Mass General Hospital, honoring their care and raising funds for the institution.

title: Cambridge pediatric doctor running fifth Boston Marathon to support Mass General patients
author: Kelly Chan
bullets: Jennifer Leah Goetz, 39, from Cambridge, is running her fifth Boston Marathon to support Mass General Hospital patients.
Goetz began running to cope with depression and trained in pediatrics and child psychiatry at Massachusetts General Hospital.
She is inspired by children with cancer and honors them by raising funds for MGH through her marathon participation.
